asset_type: Product-led TaraHome technical blog cover for an IKEA Matter-over-Thread hub compatibility guide
background_prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover background for a product-led IKEA Matter-over-Thread hub compatibility guide
Primary request: Create a clean, believable warm residential utility scene designed to receive three exact real smart-home product cutouts later. Show a broad natural-oak console shelf across the lower third, with three separate uncluttered placement areas: one for a small tabletop air-quality monitor, one for a round temperature sensor, and one for a slim door-contact sensor standing beside a simple pale-wood window frame.
Scene/backdrop: calm modern living room with warm off-white plaster, natural linen, subtle leafy plant, and a softly out-of-focus lamp; one restrained dark-green ceramic accent in the far background.
Style/medium: premium editorial interior photography, photorealistic, natural material texture and mild everyday imperfection, not CGI.
Composition/framing: landscape 3:2, eye-level three-quarter view, centered card-crop-safe focal area, oak shelf spanning the bottom third, window frame near one side without dominating the scene, enough open space for three small products.
Lighting/mood: soft late-morning window light, warm-neutral color, gentle grounded shadows, calm practical home.
Materials/textures: visible oak grain, matte plaster, woven linen, natural leaves, simple ceramic.
Constraints: background scene only; absolutely no electronics, hubs, radios, phones, cables, sensors, smart-home products, screens, text, logos, labels, diagrams, arrows, connection paths, network lines, UI, charts, translucent panels, inset frames, floating cards, progress bars, watermarks, or poster styling.
Avoid: beige AI device diagrams, futuristic lab scenes, glossy CGI, symmetrical showroom staging, fake hardware, heavy dark-green tint.
product_composite_direction: Place the exact official IKEA TIMMERFLOTTE, MYGGBETT, and ALPSTUGA press-product images on the oak surface without redrawing their shape, controls, display, cable, or proportions. Use only restrained contact shadows. Let the ALPSTUGA cable exit the frame naturally.
